title: Entadopsis polystachya
intro: Entadopsis polystachya, also known as Entada phaseoloides, is a type of leguminous vine native to tropical regions of Africa and Asia, belonging to the Fabaceae family. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including the treatment of fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. Bioactive constituents of Entadopsis polystachya include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are believed to contribute to its medicinal effects. Traditionally, the plant is used to prepare herbal remedies, such as decoctions and infusions, as well as dried extracts and tinctures, which are used to treat various health conditions.
